questions.q: What kind of being is Khara in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Khara is a rakshasa, a demon, and a kinsman of Ravana.

questions.q: Khara is described as a cousin (relative) of which demon king?
questions.explanation: Khara was a rakshasa kinsman of Ravana, the king of Lanka.

questions.q: Which region did Khara rule and guard as its demon chief?
questions.explanation: Khara was the demon ruler of Janasthana in the Dandaka forest, earning him the title 'The Demon of Janasthana'.

questions.q: Whom did Khara seek to avenge by attacking Rama?
questions.explanation: Khara attacked Rama to avenge his sister Shurpanakha, who had been disfigured.

questions.q: How large was the army Khara led against Rama?
questions.explanation: Khara led an army of fourteen thousand rakshasas against Rama.

questions.q: Who ultimately destroyed Khara and his army?
questions.explanation: Rama destroyed Khara and his entire army single-handedly.

questions.q: Shurpanakha, whom Khara sought to avenge, was disfigured by whom?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana cut off Shurpanakha's nose and ears, which led her to seek Khara's aid.

questions.q: Khara's confrontation with Rama takes place during which period of Rama's life?
options: His reign as king of Ayodhya
questions.explanation: The battle with Khara occurred while Rama was living in exile in the Dandaka forest.

questions.q: In which forest region were Khara and his demons based?
questions.explanation: Khara and his rakshasas dwelt in Janasthana within the Dandaka forest.

questions.q: What is Khara remembered for in the Ramayana?
options: Being slain alone by Rama with his vast army
Helping Rama build the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Khara is remembered as the demon who, with fourteen thousand soldiers, was destroyed by Rama alone.

questions.q: Which two demons were Khara's chief commanders who fell alongside him?
questions.explanation: Khara's principal generals Dushana and Trishira were both slain by Rama before Khara himself.

questions.q: Before facing Rama's army, Khara first sent a smaller force of how many rakshasas, who were all killed?
questions.explanation: Khara first dispatched fourteen rakshasas with Shurpanakha, and Rama slew all fourteen before the main battle.

questions.q: What did Lakshmana do with Sita while Rama fought Khara's army?
options: Took her to a hidden cave for safety
questions.explanation: Rama instructed Lakshmana to take Sita and hide in a mountain cave while he confronted the demon host.

questions.q: Which demon, Khara's general, is described as having three heads?
questions.explanation: Trishira, whose name means 'three-headed', was one of Khara's commanders slain by Rama.

questions.q: After Lakshmana disfigured Shurpanakha, to whom did she first run before Khara assembled his main army?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha first fled to her brother Khara at Janasthana, who then sent forces against Rama.

questions.q: How many rakshasas did Rama slay single-handedly in the Khara episode (the full host)?
questions.explanation: Rama destroyed the entire army of fourteen thousand rakshasas by himself.

questions.q: Which ominous portents are described as appearing as Khara marched out to battle Rama?
options: Evil omens foretelling the demons' doom
Showers of golden flowers blessing them
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes inauspicious omens accompanying Khara's march, foretelling the destruction of the rakshasas.

questions.q: In which book (kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana does the Khara episode occur?
questions.explanation: The slaying of Khara is narrated in the Aranya Kanda, the book of the forest.

questions.q: What was the broad outcome of Khara's campaign against Rama?
options: His entire army and he were annihilated
He forced Rama to flee Janasthana
He made a truce with Rama
questions.explanation: Khara's whole army was wiped out and Khara himself was killed, leaving the campaign a total failure.

questions.q: Who carried the news of Khara's death to Ravana, urging him to act?
questions.explanation: A surviving demon, Akampana, and Shurpanakha brought word of Khara's defeat to Ravana, fueling his desire for revenge.

questions.q: What weapon did Rama primarily use to destroy Khara and his forces?
questions.explanation: Rama, the great archer, used his bow and a storm of arrows to annihilate Khara's army.

questions.q: Khara's defeat is significant because it indirectly led to which major event?
options: The building of the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: The destruction of Khara so enraged and alarmed Ravana that he resolved to abduct Sita in revenge.

questions.q: According to tradition, who were Khara and his brother Dushana said to be reborn as in a later age, tied to Vishnu's vow?
options: The gatekeepers Jaya and Vijaya in one of their demon births
questions.explanation: In Vaishnava tradition, the recurring demon foes of Vishnu's avatars (such as Khara-related antagonists) are linked to the cursed gatekeepers Jaya and Vijaya across their births.

questions.q: Which charioteer-related detail marks Khara's final duel with Rama?
options: Rama shattered Khara's chariot, bow, and banner before slaying him
Khara fought entirely on foot without a chariot
Khara fled in his chariot and escaped
Rama spared Khara's chariot out of mercy
questions.explanation: In the duel Rama destroyed Khara's chariot, broke his bow and standard, and then killed him.

questions.q: With what final weapon is Rama said to have killed Khara after a fierce single combat?
options: A powerful arrow likened to Indra's thunderbolt (a celestial/aindra astra)
questions.explanation: Rama slew Khara with a blazing, irresistible arrow described as resembling the thunderbolt of Indra.

questions.q: Roughly how long is the climactic battle in which Rama destroyed Khara's host said to have lasted?
options: A short span of about an hour and a half (a fraction of a day)
A single instant with no fight
questions.explanation: Valmiki indicates Rama destroyed the fourteen thousand rakshasas in a remarkably brief period, on the order of a part of a single day.

questions.q: When Dushana charged with a mace, how did Rama deal with him?
options: Severed Dushana's arms and killed him with arrows
questions.explanation: Rama cut off Dushana's arms holding the mace and slew him with his arrows.

questions.q: Which celestial witnesses are described as gathering to watch Rama fight Khara's army alone?
options: Gods, sages, and other celestial beings in the sky
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes devas, rishis, and celestial beings assembling in the sky to behold Rama's lone stand against the demon host.

questions.q: What had the sages of Dandaka forest earlier shown Rama that foreshadowed his conflict with demons like Khara?
options: Heaps of bones of ascetics devoured by rakshasas
questions.explanation: The forest sages showed Rama the bones of hermits slain by rakshasas and begged his protection, setting the stage for his war on Khara's demons.

questions.q: Why is Rama's victory over Khara especially emphasized as extraordinary in the epic?
options: He alone defeated fourteen thousand demons plus their commanders
He used an entire vanara army to win
He defeated them through diplomacy without fighting
Lakshmana won the battle for him
questions.explanation: The episode showcases Rama single-handedly annihilating a host of fourteen thousand rakshasas along with Khara, Dushana, and Trishira.
